channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Anthropic gives its AI models limited ability to control your computer
item.description: Anthropic is giving its new Claude 3.5 Sonnet model the ability to control a user’s computer and access the internet. The move marks a major step in generative AI models’ capabilities—and raises questions about AI companies’ ability to properly mitigate the risks of more autonomous AI.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Xiaomi 15, Honor Magic 7, and more Android phones confirmed to feature the Snapdragon 8 Elite
item.description: Several Android manufacturers have confirmed that their upcoming devices will be powered by the Snapdragon 8 Elite chipset.
item.dc:creator: axel.metz@futurenet.com (Axel Metz), TechRadar

item.title: ABBA, Radiohead and The Cure musicians sign AI protest letter against 'unlicensed use' of works
item.description: Musicians from ABBA, Radiohead and The Cure have joined actors and authors in signing a protest letter against the mining of their artistry to build artificial intelligence tools.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Here’s the biggest problem with Amazon Prime Video—and how it could be fixed
item.description: October 1 is the official kickoff of spooky-movie season, but this year, we simply could not wait. On the last day of September, my wife and I watched the 2013 throwback slasher, You’re Next, one of many horror movies available to stream that I’d compiled into a seasonal list.
item.dc:creator: Joe Berkowitz, Fast Company

item.title: Big offshore wind project proposed for New York as other sites are evaluated in 3 states
item.description: BRIGANTINE, N.J. (AP) — Offshore wind energy projects in New York, New Jersey and Maryland are moving forward, as federal regulators examine the proposals and opponents escalate their legal challenges to the work.
item.dc:creator: WAYNE PARRY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: IBM launches platform to protect data from AI and quantum risks
item.description: IBM has rolled out a new platform to help enterprise customers better handle the complicated data workflows and security risks that arise when managing AI deployments, hybrid cloud infrastructure, and quantum computing systems.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: We need a national STEM Act to stem the risk of a declining America
item.description: Wars have unintended consequences, and positive results can emerge from tragedy. For proof, just look at the list from NATO of some unexpected outcomes of R&D that led to society-changing benefits.
item.dc:creator: Frederic Bertley, Fast Company

item.title: How the Army makes software a front and center focus
item.description: "One of the things that we really change as part of the software implementation is, no longer is the Army going to retest everything," said Jennifer Swanson. The post How the Army makes software a front and center focus first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: The Dark Web of Disinformation: Russia's Viral Campaign Against Democratic Candidates
item.description: Russian disinformation targeting US Democratic candidates, including viral videos and online propaganda
item.dc:creator: David Klepper, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: You’re going to have a robot coworker sooner than you think
item.description: They marched in line, interacted with guests, and poured drinks while making small talk. The vision of the future Elon Musk put forward with his Optimus humanoid robots at Tesla’s recent Cybercab event earlier this month was as astounding as it was unbelievable.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Trump vs. Harris: The Battle for Young Voters on Snapchat
item.description: Kamala Harris's big bet on Snapchat and Trump's absence from the platform reveal a growing divide in youth engagement.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Two-thirds of leaders feel genAI will reshape entry-level jobs
item.description: Generative AI is on track to transform entry-level roles across industries, with 64% of leaders expecting these positions to evolve from creation to review and refinement of outputs within the next three years.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Aragón's Leap in Innovation: A Plan for Competitive Technological Leadership
item.description: Aragón, Spain, has unveiled a comprehensive innovation plan to boost its competitiveness and position itself as a technological leader.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: FTC's rule banning fake online reviews goes into effect
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal rule banning fake online reviews is now in effect. The Federal Trade Commission issued the rule in August banning the sale or purchase of online reviews.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Stanford researchers map ‘White-Only’ properties using AI
item.description: Racially restrictive covenants are clauses in property deeds that ban individuals of a particular race from buying a home.
item.dc:creator: Hamza Fahmy, NewsNation

item.title: Judge delays order in antitrust case requiring Google to open up its app store
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — A federal judge on Friday delayed an order requiring Google to open up its Android app store to more competition until an appeals court decides whether to block the shake-up because of legal questions surrounding a jury's verdict that branded Google as an illegal monopolist.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 6 key mobile and IoT/OT attack trend findings
item.description: The role of the CISO continues to expand, driven by the rising number of breaches and cyberattacks like ransomware, as well as SEC requirements for public organizations to disclose material breaches.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: 7 Android 15 features you can bring to any phone today
item.description: No Android 15? No problem. You can still enjoy some of the fancy new features from Google’s latest Android version — no matter who made your current device or how old it might be.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Hit the lights: Mets outfit Citi Field for electric light shows
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Mets are lighting up Citi Field, even before their batters step up to the plate. About 70 front-office staffers scattered around the ballpark for over five hours to install 3,500 strips of LED lights on each railing ahead of New York's first home postseason game.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How to use Task.WhenEach in .NET 9
item.description: The upcoming release of .NET 9 introduces the Task.WhenEach method, which enables developers to handle asynchronous tasks more elegantly. The Task.WhenEach method allows us to iterate through tasks as they complete, without having to call Task.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: US agency adopts rule to make it easier for consumers to cancel unwanted subscriptions
item.description: The Federal Trade Commission adopted a final rule Wednesday that will require businesses to make it easy for consumers to cancel unwanted subscriptions and memberships.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
